mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units sold signals an expansion in aggregate demand that boosts equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production signals a contraction in supply capacity that compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in supply-side shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that raises discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in early-cycle contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income signals an expansion in aggregate demand that boosts equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production and a 1-month decline in housing units sold signal a contraction in supply capacity that compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply-side shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate and a 2-month increase in the consumer price index signal a tightening monetary cycle that raises discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims and a 1-month increase in the unemployment rate signal a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in early-cycle contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
